title: Auxiliary laps-to-next-stop target with probability-horizon consistency
summary: A recent pit-stop paper reports a multi-task setup where next-lap classification is helped by a horizon regression head.
rationale: A recent pit-stop paper reports a multi-task setup where next-lap classification is helped by a horizon regression head. This directly matches your label structure and is a sharper version of generic auxiliary supervision because the two outputs can be tied by discrete-time consistency.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary target `laps_until_next_pit` within each driver-race sequence. Train a shared model with binary `PitNextLap` plus ordinal/bucketed horizon prediction; at inference, feed OOF horizon logits or expected remaining laps into the main model, or enforce consistency so shorter predicted horizons imply higher next-lap risk.
expected_effect: Can reduce myopic errors where rows are correctly identified as 'pit soon' but misranked within the final few laps before the stop.
risk: Auxiliary labels are censored at race end and after missing stops; noisy construction can hurt more than help unless masking/censor handling is careful.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
